title: Cross-Border IP Masking with Domestic Card Presentation
narrative: Fraudster uses VPN or proxy to mask true location while presenting stolen US-issued card with legitimate domestic billing address. Card holder's genuine pattern shows consistent US transactions over 180+ days, then suddenly switches to IP addresses from high-risk jurisdictions (Romania, Nigeria, Indonesia) while billing addr1 remains unchanged. Transactions spike from typical $45-120 range to $380-850 per transaction within 48 hours. The dist1 feature (distance between billing and IP location) jumps from historical 0-50km baseline to 8000+ km. Perpetrator targets digital goods (ProductCD='W') and gift cards to monetize quickly before detection, maintaining CVV match to appear legitimate but failing AVS street number check.
indicators: dist1 metric increases from <50km baseline to >7500km across 2-4 consecutive transactions
IP country changes to high-risk jurisdiction while billing addr1 remains constant US value
TransactionAmt increases 4-7x above cardholder's 90-day rolling average within 24-48 hours
card1_txn_count_24h reaches 6-12 transactions versus typical 0-2 daily baseline
ProductCD shifts exclusively to 'W' (digital) or 'C' (cash equivalent) from mixed historical pattern
CVV match=1 but AVS returns partial match (zip only, street mismatch)
Transactions cluster in is_night=1 time windows (2-6 AM) in stated billing timezone
summary: A US-issued card suddenly transacts from IP addresses 7,500+ km away in high-risk countries while the billing address stays unchanged, with transaction amounts jumping 4-7x above normal and clustering on digital goods or gift cards.
reasoning: The fraudster uses stolen card credentials with a VPN to hide their true foreign location, rushing to extract value through quickly monetizable digital products before the legitimate cardholder notices the unauthorized charges.
indicator_explanations: Card suddenly appears thousands of miles away from its normal geographic footprint
Connection originates from risky foreign country while billing address stays domestic
Purchase amounts spike dramatically beyond what this cardholder typically spends
Card generates a flurry of transactions well above its normal daily activity
Purchases pivot entirely to digital goods and cash-like products from varied history
CVV is correct but street address fails verification against billing records
All activity concentrates in middle-of-night hours for the cardholder's stated timezone
